visits.note: Initial visit for new patient with complaints of fatigue and weight loss. Physical examination reveals a BP of 140/90 and a BMI of 28. Diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes and Hyperlipidemia. Start medications Metformin 500mg BID and Atorvastatin 20mg QD.

Follow-up visit 4 months after initial diagnosis. Patient reports improvement in fatigue but still experiencing weight loss. Physical examination reveals a BP of 138/92. Lab results show worsening HbA1c to 8.1. Diagnosis of Hypertension added. Increase Metformin dose to 1000mg BID and start Lisinopril 10mg QD.

Follow-up visit 6 months after V2. Patient reports continued weight loss and edema. Physical examination reveals a BP of 145/98 and a BMI of 30. Lab results show worsening eGFR to 58. Diagnosis of Chronic Kidney Disease added. Increase Lisinopril dose to 20mg QD and start Furosemide 40mg BID.

Follow-up visit 3 months after V3. Patient reports improvement in edema and shortness of breath. Physical examination reveals a BP of 142/95. Lab results show improvement in eGFR to 62. Dose adjustment of Furosemide to 20mg BID.

Follow-up visit 6 months after V4. Patient reports continued weight loss but no shortness of breath. Physical examination reveals a BP of 135/80. Lab results show improvement in HbA1c to 7.8 and LDL to 100. Consideration of statin intensification.
